content: Called Acme Corp procurement lead (Sarah Chen) about audit logging gaps. She flagged that their compliance officer won't sign off without immutable event trails. This is blocking a $180K annual contract.
summary: Acme Corp: audit logs blocking $180K deal

content: Pattern: when customers say 'we need better audit trails,' they usually mean 'we need to prove governance to our legal/compliance teams so we feel safe deploying this in prod.' Audit logs aren't a feature—they're a permission slip.
summary: Audit logs = customer safety/governance validation, not feature

content: Synthesized November feedback from 4 customer interviews (Acme, Initech, Globex, Wayne Industries). Common thread: all cite audit/compliance as top 3 blocker. Wrote 2-pager for Sebastien + Lina.
summary: Nov synthesis: audit logs mentioned by 4/4 customers

content: Monthly customer feedback loop: (1) book 4–5 calls 2nd–3rd week, (2) record + transcribe, (3) Wednesday synthesis doc (async for Lina/Sebastien), (4) tag blockers/personas in Linear. Takes ~6 hrs/month. Calendar reminder on the 10th.
summary: Process: book→record→synthesize→tag (2nd–3rd week each month)

content: Anya pointed out in standup: Initech's CFO wanted 'faster ROI visibility'—but actually wanted reassurance that implementation wouldn't derail their Q1 roadmap. Reframed our pitch to them accordingly.
summary: Initech CFO's real concern: implementation risk, not speed

content: Anya's insight holds: 'I want X' is usually 'I want to feel Y.' Globex said 'we need better integrations'—really meant 'we need to feel confident we won't be locked in.' Shapes how we pitch.
summary: Customer requests mask emotional needs (security, control, speed)

content: Wayne Industries CSM escalated: their data team wants SLA guarantees on query latency. Marcus confirmed we can't commit <2sec for 99th percentile at scale. This is a real gap, not perception.
summary: Wayne Industries: SLA request legitimate, not overblown

content: Audit logs + SLA guarantees are two sides of the same coin: enterprise buyers want proof that our system is mature enough to bet prod workloads on. Both are 'maturity proxies,' not nice-to-haves.
summary: Audit logs & SLAs = maturity signals for enterprise risk tolerance

content: December feedback call with Globex (3 attendees: procurement, eng, product). They praised onboarding but said data residency questions stalled their legal review by 2 weeks. Not our fault, but we should have FAQ.
summary: Globex: data residency questions delayed legal review

content: When synthesizing feedback, always map to persona + blocker (in Linear label format 'persona:finance' / 'blocker:audit-logs'). Makes it searchable for Lina/Sebastien. Takes extra 10 min, worth it.
summary: Tag feedback: persona + blocker in Linear (searchable)

content: Called Acme Corp again (late Jan). Sarah Chen now says compliance sign-off still pending but audit log roadmap from our last call 'moved the needle.' Deal now ETA March vs. blocked. Progress.
summary: Acme Corp: audit log roadmap commitment unblocked deal

content: Customer interviews often reveal timing mismatch: we say 'audit logs in Q2,' they hear 'we don't care about your January deadline.' Solution: be explicit about what 'Q2' means for their budget cycle.
summary: Timing objections often stem from misaligned calendar expectations

content: Initech product lead flagged that they're comparing us to Anthropic's memory layer. We don't compete directly, but it signals they're evaluating embedded vs. standalone. Noted for Lina.
summary: Initech: competitive concern re embedded memory solutions

content: Competitive pressure is low across customer base, but Anthropic/Voyage AI are mentioned as 'what if we just use their API?' Differentiation: we're persistence layer, they're embedding models. Message needs tightening.
summary: Perception gap: customers conflate embedding models with memory

content: February synthesis: 5 calls (Acme, Initech, Globex, Wayne Industries, + new prospect Cyberdyne). Cyberdyne asked about multi-tenant data isolation. Valid question; Devon flagged it's handled but poorly documented.
summary: Feb feedback: multi-tenant isolation docs gap identified

content: After each monthly synthesis, send 15-min async video walkthrough to Sebastien (not just doc). Context sticks better. Lina watches async too. Use Loom, link in Slack. Saves 30 min of sync meetings.
summary: Post-synthesis: async Loom walkthrough to Sebastien/Lina

content: Wayne Industries escalated: they want us to fund on-premise deployment pilot ($50K cost). Anya flagged this is outside our commercial model. I told them Q3 timeline, but relationship risk is real.
summary: Wayne Industries: on-prem request out of scope, relationship strained

content: Realized I dread the synthesis work when feedback is contradictory (audit logs vs. speed) because I want customers to have a clear ask. But ambiguity is real; my job is to *name* the tension, not resolve it for product.
summary: I resist documenting customer ambiguity; need to reframe as signal

content: Enterprise personas (finance, legal, engineering) have misaligned priorities. Finance wants ROI proof + SLAs. Legal wants audit trails + compliance. Engineering wants low ops burden. All three must sign off. Shape messaging to each.
summary: Multi-stakeholder sign-off requires persona-specific messaging

content: Late April: Acme Corp deal closed at $180K annual (signed ~4 weeks after audit log roadmap commit). Marcus said onboarding is smooth. This validates the 'audit logs = permission slip' hypothesis.
summary: Acme Corp: $180K closed after audit log roadmap commit
